title: Uni file
author: jamal
text:
Uni file

jamal

network pdf

[Page 1/4] TD Managing Users and Groups Q1. Sometimes instructions say that I must “be root.” What does this mean? Q2. What is the root account and how does it differ from a user account? Q3. Why would I want to add other user accounts to my system? Q4. How do I delete users? Q5. Is there any way to change a user ID? Q6. Is there a way to disable a user account? Q7. How do I add or change personal information in an account? Q8. How do I change a password? Exercise 1 1. Create some users and stageY totox giving passwords and stageypass totoxpss 2. Audits: an immediate opportunity to log in these accounts, setting up their rep. staff / home. 3. Try to create an existing account. 4. Delete without regret on behalf of totox. His rep. staff he was removed? Exercise 2 It is about creating a group named trainee whose members are the accounts stageX. As root : 1. create the student group: add some accounts stageX, foo1, foo2, toto3 in this group 2. check the result with the control groups Exercise 3 What does the following commands: - # Useradd Fadi - # Useradd-e 19-9-2010 fadi - # Useradd-g fadihome fadi - # Useradd-g-d touficf grit Toufic - # Useradd-c "Bernard's Account"-d / home / .bernard-e "2003-08-31" - # Userdel bernard - # Userdel-r bernard - # Usermod tony Paschal - # Usermod-G root, staff, operator-e 2013-12-31 pascal - # Groupadd-g 666 gnu - # 667 gnu-g groupmod - # Groupdel gnu - # Usermod-d pascalfol Paschal - # Usermod-d-m pascalfol Paschal - # Usermod-l Paschal - # Usermod-u Paschal Exercise 4 What do the commands in / etc / sudoers: - Kamal host1 = / sbin / shutdown - Kamil ALL = ALL [Page 2/4] The main orders useradd, usermod, userdel user account management groupadd, groupmod, groupdel group management pwck, grpck file verification passwd change the password of a user chfn, id, groups, finger Misc Options: -U uid to set uid -G group-primary fixed membership user to a list g-list secondary groups (separator, no spaces) -S shell default award of default shell bash -C comment -D dir. staff default in the directory / home E-expiration-date fixed expiration date of the account (MM / DD / YY) -M to create the directory K skel-dir- copies the contents of the rep-skel dir. personnel, by default / etc / skel SUDO sudo allows you to run programs with root privileges. We can therefore give permission to a user or group of users to run programs with root privileges (for exam halt or reboot), in the case of a server with multiple users this can be handy. You can also use sudo, if it was just too lazy to raise its rights with su. sudo is installed by default on Ubuntu. / Etc / sudoers The sudo configuration is done from the file / Etc / sudoers Its edition is through the command visudo which is in fact an improved text editor for sudo (like crontab). [Page 3/4] To create the user stageX, Root passes the command: useradd stageX This creates: o home directory / Home / stageX, With the default account name o a new entry in two basic file / etc / passwd and / etc / group. o To know Options useradd (required to manage accounts by using scripts) ● To assign a password: passwd stageX enter 2 times stgx ● Delete a user account (not connected) at random .. totox. userdel [-r] totox Option -R also removes the dir. staff and user files The command removes all traces of the user in the configuration file: / Etc / passwd including groups of users only. ● Change the user account foo usermod [options] totox The options are the same that useradd usermod-G student, teacher stageX stageX added in the 2 groups and trainee teachers (which must exist) First form of the useradd command: Summoned in this way, the command creates a new user account using the values specified (on the command line) and values system default if the options are not specified. Depending on the options chosen, the new account will be created and files will be copied in its original workspace. SYNOPSIS: useradd-c comment-d homedir expire_date-e-f-g inactive_date initial_group - -G group,-m-k-p skel_dir passwd-u uid-s shell-ologin EXAMPLE: add a user. tux: / # useradd-c "Bernard's Account"-d / home / .bernard-e "2003-08-31" - - - # Usermod-d pascalfol Paschal we change the home directory of a Paschal pascalfol - # Usermod-d-m pascalfol Paschal is changing and moving the home directory of Easter to pascalfol (copy information) - # Usermod-l Paschal deactivate the password for Easter - # Usermod-u Paschal Enable password for Easter usermod usermod command to modify the characteristics of a user. SYNOPSIS: usermod-c comment-d homedir-me expire_date inactive_date-f-g - initial_group-group G,-l loginname-p passwd-u uid-s shell-ol-Ulogin Example: Add user in the operator group Paschal (It is already member of the main root gnu and groups and staff) and he set an expiration date to 31.12.2013: [Page 4/4] tux: / # usermod-G root, staff, operator-e 2013-12-31 pascal userdel The command userdel removes a user account (if any). Option -R allows in addition to the removal of the contents of the directory and debit of the user, and its mail directory. SYNOPSIS: userdel-rlogin Example: Remove an undefined users tux: / # userdel-r bernard groupadd The command groupadd creates a new group. SYNOPSIS: groupadd-g gid-ologin EXAMPLE: Add Group tux: / # groupadd-g 666 gnu groupmod The command groupmod can change the group settings. SYNOPSIS: groupmod-g gid-o-n group_namegroup EXAMPLE: Modfier gid group gnu. tux: / # groupmod 667 gnu-g groupdel The command groupdel, Takes a group (if any). SYNOPSIS: groupdelgroup Example: Remove the group gnu. tux: / # groupdel gnu

title: jj
author: gh
text:
jj

gh

td1

[Page 1/2] TD Process Management and monitoring Exercise 1: What do the following commands: - Top - Ps-ax - Ps-l - Ps ax | grep squid - kill - Kill -9 1134 - Killall-HUP squid - kill-STOP pid - Gedit or vi / samir/f1 & - At - Atk - Atrm Exercise 2 Pid user tty command NI PRI 555 karim --- gedit / etc / shadow 5 75 P1 566 Salim --- gedit / ssss/f1 5 55 P2 577 hani --- vi /sami/f3 588 hiba --- gedit / bibi/f13 599 lina --- http Give the commands required to: 1- Kill process nb 555 2- Kill all the processes of the command gedit (i.e 555 566 588) 3- Force process 599 to reread its configuration file 4- Let p2 comes first Exercise 3 Pid user tty command NI PRI 555 salim --- gedit / ssss/f1 10 55 666 salim --- gedit / ssss/f2 5 75 What is the command that salim (usually undefined user) should use to reverse the priorities of the above two processes. Exercise 4 Complete the table: Orders Description ps ps aux | grep squid pstree kill killall kill -9 1134 killall -1 squid kill-CONT 1134 kill-STOP 1134 kill-l [Page 2/2] - Info ps list running processes and with the options -To, provides information: who launched what resources it uses, its condition, its name, PID (a unique number identifying each). A process that does not respond is represented by the letter 'Z' (zombie) by the command ps aux. A process normally inactive is represented by the letter 'S' (sleep). - Info killFollowed by a process name or PID closed properly process. If it fails because there are problem kill -9 'Kill' the process. The combination ctrl-c also works (also used ctrl-l to refresh the view). This helps to stop a program that has stopped responding, without rebooting. It is generally not necessary to reboot to fix a problem. - Info killall closes a set of processes. pstree displays the hierarchical tree of processes, to find the parent process of a given process. Depending on the roles, processes are initiated only when they are called, others are permanently present, they are called 'demons'. Start, stop, restart is done by a demon / Etc / init.d / <script> <action>, The action is the choice start, stop, restart, reload to keep the cache.

author: hadi

Exercise 4 Complete the table: Orders Description ps ps aux | grep squid pstree kill killall kill -9 1134 killall -1 squid kill-CONT 1134 kill-STOP 1134 kill-l [Page 2/2] - Info ps list running processes and with the options -To, provides information: who launched what resources it uses, its condition, its name, PID (a unique number identifying each). A process that does not respond is represented by the letter 'Z' (zombie) by the command ps aux. A process normally inactive is represented by the letter 'S' (sleep). - Info killFollowed by a process name or PID closed properly process. If it fails because there are problem kill -9 'Kill' the process. The combination ctrl-c also works (also used ctrl-l to refresh the view). Depending on the roles, processes are initiated only when they are called, others are permanently present, they are called 'demons'. title: Ai
author: nano
text:
Ai

nano

artificial intelligence

[Page 1/20] Network Administration Under LINUX PROCESS MANAGEMENT 2021-2022 1 [Page 2/20] INTRODUCTION When you install Linux, or when you want to install a new program, you have to choose which ones to start and how to start them. Finally afterwards the question is how I stop it, how I modify its configuration, how I know if it is running. A program is just a simple file in which you put commands. When you launch this program it will be taken over by the system. The environment program couple becomes a process. Linux being a multitasking system, several programs can run at the same time, which requires that the program be identified and that the system organize a distribution of resources between the different programs. Finally, when a program is started, it must be possible to continue working, it is therefore necessary to be able to launch this program in the background. 2021-2022 2 [Page 3/20] VISUALIZE THE PROCESSES: THE PS COMMAND ps command: ps gives the list of processes, The options The "-x" option allows you to view all the active processes of the current user The "-ax" option allows you to view all the processes of the machine of all the users The "-aux" option allows you to view displays the users associated with each process The "-u username" option displays each process associated with a user The "-p PID" option displays information about a process The "-t" option displays all the processes associated with a terminal 2021-2022 3 [Page 4/20] VISUALIZE THE PROCESSES 2021-2022 4 [Page 5/20] VISUALIZE THE PROCESSES USER indicates the name of the user of the process PID is the unique identifier of the process that has been defined by the system % CPU displays the CPU usage as a percentage % MEM displays RAM usage as a percentage VSZ gives the usage of shared libraries and the memory used for its operation RSS gives the physical memory usage used in kilobytes by the process (excluding swap) TTY indicates the terminal port number (the "?" Indicates that the command is not associated with a terminal) STAT displays the current status of the process, R for Run Start ... START indicates the time at which the process started TIME displays the processor time used by this process CMD displays the name of the command that uses this PID 2021-2022 5 [Page 6/20] VISUALIZE THE PROCESSES RSS is the resident-defined size and is used to indicate the amount of memory allocated to this process that is in RAM. It does not include memory that is swap memory. It includes the memory of shared libraries as long as the pages of those libraries are actually in memory. It includes all stack and heap memory. VSZ is the size of virtual memory. It includes all memory that the process can access, including memory that is swapped, memory allocated but not used, and memory from shared libraries. 2021-2022 6 [Page 7/20] DETAIL OF STAT FIELD D Uninterruptible from sleep R Executable start S Waits for event to be completed, asleep (sleep) T Stopped, either by job command signal or because it is traced X Dead (should normally never be seen) Z Process "zombie" 2021-2022 7 [Page 8/20] VISUALIZE THE PROCESSES The top command: We also use the top command to see what is running on the machine. If we try the "top" command, we will have: we will have the processes and we see in real time how things change 2021-2022 8 [Page 9/20] VISUALIZE THE PROCESSES By default, a process is started in the foreground running state. It can be stopped with the Ctrl + C combination, in which case it will be destroyed. But we can also send it in the background. If we run it from the start with an &, it will be in the running state in the background. If we choose to do Ctrl + Z, it will go to the Stopped state in the background. You will have to type bg to switch it back to the running state in the background. Finally, the fg command returns a process from the background to the foreground. Take five minutes to properly analyze this diagram and verify that you have understood the gist of this chapter, it is really important. It pretty much sums up everything you need to know. It only lacks nohup that I set aside like I told you 2021-2022 9 [Page 10/20] BACKGROUND PROCESSES To start a background process, start it with the & option at the end. Take the example of top, which allows you to follow the processes running on your machine. If you run top with nothing, you can't do anything on the console you were on 2021-2022 10 [Page 11/20] BACKGROUND PROCESSES To start a background process, start it with the & option at the end. Take the example of top, which allows you to follow the processes running on your machine. If you run top with nothing, you can't do anything on the console you were on To see that it is in the background, use the jobs command. You then get the list of programs that you have placed in the background. 2021-2022 11 [Page 12/20] BRINGING PROCESSES BACK TO THE FRONT If you now want to bring this program back to the foreground type fg or fg% 1 (I'm assuming here the job number is 1, you can get it with the jobs command Now you have already started your program, and you want to send it to the background. You must first suspend it using CTRL Z then bg or bg% 1 to send it to the background. 2021-2022 12 [Page 13/20] THE KILL AND KILLALL COMMANDS kill Allows a signal to be sent to the process using its PID number killall Allows you to send a signal to the process using its name. 2021-2022 13 [Page 14/20] THE KILL AND KILLALL COMMANDS Orders Options Notes a: To see also the processes of other users x: To see the process all terminals Displays an instantaneous state of ps l: long listing running processes u: For the user name and start time f: displays the tree of parent and child processes. ps aux | grep Note the use of the | (pipe) to send the result of a command to another Displays only the lines containing squid squid command provides information on the connection pstree Ps command nearly equivalent axf (f as filiation) between the process Sends a signal to the process using its kill PID number Sends a signal to the process by using killall its name. kills the process whose PID number kill -9 1134 You can use squid killall -9 1134 Requires squid to read the file killall -1 squid killall-HUP squid or kill-HUP 1134 confiuration Reactive process found in the kill-CONT 1134 background kill-STOP 1134 Suspend a background process kill-l displays all signals available 2021-2022 14 [Page 15/20] THE KILL AND KILLALL COMMANDS lists the programs running in the jobs background Enter the number task if you have more than a% bg A program in the background You get this number with the command jobs fg Replace a program to the foreground Same as bg init To change the runlevel To move from level 3 (default) in a type init 1 runlevel To know the level in which you are Display the pid of a process. Used in pidof pidof is actually a symbolic link killall5 scripts. - List For a list of processes that are started or stopped. Allows you to create or delete symbolic - Add to add squid squid chkconfig links to start or stop a process with the - Del to remove squid squid (the script file located in / etc / rc.d / init.d / is system V not affected). Enables you to find listening ports on your machine. For example, if you must netstat-na have sendmail running on port 25 to listen. 2021-2022 15 [Page 16/20] PROCESS PLANNING Process planning at: Launch of commands at the specified time. atq: Display of the list of jobs waiting to be executed. atrm: Deleting a job waiting to be executed 2021-2022 16 [Page 17/20] PROCESS PRIORITY Allows you to modify the priority of a process when it is launched. Only the super- Nice user is able to lower the value, thus increasing the priority of a process (relative value). Allows you to modify the priority of a running process, be careful to give an Renice absolute value. 2021-2022 17 [Page 18/20] PROCESS PRIORITY 2021-2022 18 [Page 19/20] PROCESS PRIORITY 2021-2022 19 [Page 20/20] PROCESS PRIORITY To reduce the priority: Note the use of two "-" characters. To make an app more selfish and less enjoyable, you have to use sudo. Anyone can make their request more enjoyable, but only super-users can lower the priority. 2021-2022 20
